title: 10 best herbal teas for eye bags

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Kansas Coneflower tea has anti-inflammatory compounds that help reduce puffiness and swelling under the eyes, alleviating the appearance of eye bags.

Antioxidant properties: The antioxidants present in Kansas Coneflower tea help combat free radicals that can contribute to dark circles and discoloration around the eyes, promoting a healthier and more radiant complexion.

Cooling and soothing properties: The cooling effects of Kansas Coneflower tea can help reduce redness and irritation, while its soothing properties can calm and comfort the delicate skin around the eyes.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Echinacea angustifolia, also known as Kansas coneflower, teas helps with eye bags because of its potent antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties. The tea's active compounds, such as alkylamides and polyacetylenes, help to reduce puffiness and swelling around the eyes. Additionally, Echinacea's ability to boost the immune system and fight off infection may help to alleviate eye bag causes like allergies, sinus pressure, and fatigue. Regular consumption of this herbal tea may lead to a refreshed and rejuvenated appearance around the eyes.
remedy_constituents: Iridoid glycosides: These compounds help reduce puffiness and inflammation associated with eye bags by exhibiting anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties.
Phenolic acids (including chlorogenic acid): These antioxidants help minimize oxidative stress and inflammation that contribute to eye bag formation, promoting a more even and rested appearance.
Alkaloids (including isobutylamides): These compounds may aid in reducing fluid retention and inflammation, helping to minimize the appearance of eye bags.
remedy_parts: Roots: They are rich in anti-inflammatory compounds, which help reduce puffiness and swelling in eye bags.
Leaves: Rich in antioxidants, they can help soothe and calm the skin around the eyes, reducing the appearance of eye bags.
Flowers: They contain soothing and anti-inflammatory properties that can help alleviate eye bag discomfort and promote relaxation.
remedy_recipe: Harvest echinacea angustifolia roots in late fall or early spring when the plant is dormant for maximum potency.
Clean the dried roots to remove any debris or dirt using a fine-mesh sieve and water.
Dry the cleaned roots in a low-temperature oven at 150 degrees fahrenheit for 2 hours.
Steep one teaspoon of dried echinacea angustifolia root in 8 ounces of boiling water for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea and discard the solids to enjoy a warm and soothing cup of echinacea angustifolia tea.

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Oat tea contains anti-inflammatory agents like avenanthramides, which can reduce puffiness and swelling in the eyes, helping to alleviate eye bag discomfort.

Antioxidant properties: Oats are rich in antioxidants that help neutralize free radicals and protect the delicate skin around the eyes from damage, reducing the appearance of dark circles and eye bags.
Soften and moisturize skin: Oat tea's soothing and moisturizing properties can help soften and calm irritated or inflamed skin under the eyes, reducing redness and puffiness.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Avena sativa, also known as oats, teas helps with eye bags because of its soothing and anti-inflammatory properties. The saponins present in oats can help to reduce puffiness and swelling, while its antioxidant properties combat free radicals that cause dark circles. The calming effects of Avena sativa can also help to reduce stress and fatigue, common causes of eye bags. As a result, Avena sativa teas can provide a natural and effective solution for reducing the appearance of eye bags and promoting a more refreshed and rejuvenated look.
remedy_constituents: Avenacosides: Avenacosides are flavonoid glycosides that have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce puffiness and swelling associated with eye bags.
Saponins: Saponins in Avena sativa tea have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects, which can help soothe and calm the skin around the eyes, reducing the appearance of eye bags.
Silicic acid: Silicic acid in Avena sativa tea has been shown to improve skin elasticity and firmness, which can help reduce the appearance of eye bags and dark circles.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Rich in antioxidants and flavonoids, the leaves of Avena sativa help to reduce puffiness and inflammation associated with eye bags.
Seeds: The seeds of Avena sativa contain a compound called avenanthramides, which has anti-inflammatory properties that help to reduce swelling and soothe eye bags.
Flowers: The flowers of Avena sativa are used to make teas that help to reduce puffiness and inflammation, and are also rich in antioxidants that promote overall eye health.
remedy_recipe: Measure one teaspoon of dried avena sativa root per cup of boiling water for a standard serving size.
Heat the measured avena sativa root in 8 ounces of boiling water for about 5 to 7 minutes.
Strain the infused liquid through a fine-mesh sieve into a cup to remove the root particles.
Allow the strained tea to steep for an additional 2 to 3 minutes to enhance its flavor and aroma.
Discard the spent root particles and serve the avena sativa tea immediately while still warm.

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Pot marigold tea contains flavonoids and terpenes, which have anti-inflammatory effects that can reduce puffiness and swelling under the eyes.

Antioxidant properties: The antioxidants in pot marigold tea, such as carotenoids and lutein, help protect the delicate skin around the eyes from environmental stressors and damage caused by free radicals, reducing the appearance of dark circles and puffiness.

Cooling and soothing effects: Pot marigold tea has a calming and cooling effect on the skin, which can help reduce redness, irritation, and swelling associated with eye bags, making it an effective remedy for tired-looking eyes.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Calendula officinalis, also known as pot marigold, teas helps with eye bags because of its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The flavonoids present in Calendula officinalis reduce puffiness and swelling, while its antiseptic properties help to combat bacteria and other microorganisms that can cause eye irritation. Additionally, the tea's soothing properties calm and relax the skin around the eyes, reducing the appearance of eye bags and promoting a more rested and refreshed look. This can leave the eyes looking smoother and more radiant.
remedy_constituents: Triterpenoids: These compounds in Calendula officinalis have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce the puffiness and swelling associated with eye bags.

Flavonoids: Flavonoids in Calendula officinalis have antioxidant properties that can help combat free radicals and reduce oxidative stress, leading to reduced inflammation and swelling in the under-eye area.
Phenylpropanoids: These compounds in Calendula officinalis have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help reduce inflammation, swelling, and discoloration in the under-eye area.
remedy_parts: Flowers: They are used due to their high content of calendulin and triterpenoids, which provide anti-inflammatory and soothing properties beneficial for eye bags.
Leaves: They contain flavonoids and other compounds that contribute to their ability to reduce swelling and promote relaxation in the eye area when consumed as a tea.
Stems: The stems of Calendula officinalis have antioxidant properties, which can help in reducing puffiness and inflammation when used in tea for eye bags.
remedy_recipe: Harvest the flowers of calendula officinalis by cutting them in the morning after the dew has dried.
Dry the flowers in a low-temperature oven at 105 degrees fahrenheit for 2 hours.
Grind 1 teaspoon of dried flowers into a fine powder using a spice grinder.
Steep 1 teaspoon of the ground flowers in 1 cup of boiling water for 5 to 7 minutes.
Strain the liquid and discard the solids before serving the tea.

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Dandelion tea contains flavonoids, which have been shown to reduce inflammation and alleviate puffiness under the eyes, making it an effective remedy for eye bags.

Diuretic properties: The diuretic effects of dandelion tea help to reduce water retention in the body, which can contribute to puffy eyes and dark circles, by promoting urination and flushing out excess fluids.

Antioxidant properties: Dandelion tea's rich antioxidant content helps to protect the skin under the eyes from environmental stressors and damage, reducing the appearance of fine lines and wrinkles, and giving a more rested and refreshed look.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Taraxacum officinale, also known as dandelion, teas helps with eye bags because it contains antioxidants and diuretic properties that work together to reduce water retention and puffiness. The tea's anti-inflammatory compounds help to soothe and calm the skin around the eyes, while its diuretic properties facilitate the removal of excess fluids, reducing the appearance of eye bags. Regular consumption of dandelion tea may also help to improve circulation and promote overall skin health, resulting in a more radiant and refreshed appearance.
remedy_constituents: Saponins: Saponins in Taraxacum officinale tea have anti-inflammatory properties, which help reduce puffiness and swelling under the eyes associated with eye bags.

Flavonoids: Flavonoids, particularly quercetin and kaempferol, in Taraxacum officinale tea exhibit antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects, which contribute to reducing eye bag appearance and alleviating related discomfort.

Taraxasterol: Taraxasterol, a triterpene saponin, has been shown to possess anti-inflammatory properties, helping to reduce inflammation and swelling associated with eye bags, thus providing relief from eye bag discomfort.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves are commonly used to make teas due to their anti-inflammatory properties, which help reduce puffiness and swelling under the eyes.
Flowers: The flowers of Taraxacum officinale are used to make teas that can help to reduce water retention and alleviate eye strain, contributing to the alleviation of eye bags.
Roots: The roots of Taraxacum officinale are used to make teas that can help to reduce fluid retention and alleviate puffiness, making them effective in addressing eye bags.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh taraxacum officinale leaves and flowers by cutting them at the base with scissors during the spring.
Clean the harvested taraxacum officinale leaves and flowers by gently rinsing them with cold water for 5 minutes.
Combine 1 tablespoon of dried taraxacum officinale leaves and flowers with 1 cup of boiling water in a teapot.
Steep the taraxacum officinale mixture for 5-7 minutes or until the liquid reaches your desired strength.
Strain the taraxacum officinale tea into a cup using a fine-mesh sieve and discard the solids.

remedy_properties: Hyperforin: This compound has anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce puffiness and swelling under the eyes caused by eye bags.

Flavonoids: The flavonoids present in St John's Wort tea have antioxidant properties, which can help soothe and calm the skin around the eyes, reducing inflammation and promoting circulation to alleviate dark circles and eye bags.

Volatile oils: The volatile oils in St John's Wort tea, such as limonene and citral, have a mild antibacterial and antifungal effect, which can help prevent infections that may contribute to puffiness and swelling around the eyes.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Hypericum perforatum, also known as St. John's Wort, teas helps with eye bags because of its potent antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties. The tea's flavonoids and phenolic acids work to reduce puffiness and swelling around the eyes, while its tannins help to tighten and tone the skin. Additionally, the tea's ability to improve circulation and reduce water retention contributes to its effectiveness in alleviating eye bags and promoting a more refreshed and rejuvenated appearance. remedy_constituents: Hyperforin: This terpenoid helps reduce inflammation and swelling associated with eye bags, promoting a more even and rested appearance of the under-eye area.

Quercetin: A flavonoid with potent antioxidant properties, Quercetin helps combat oxidative stress and inflammation that can lead to puffiness and discoloration of the under-eye area, contributing to the appearance of eye bags.

Kaempferol: A flavonol with anti-inflammatory properties, Kaempferol helps alleviate swelling and redness associated with eye bags, promoting a smoother and more even-toned appearance of the under-eye area.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are used due to their anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce puffiness and swelling in the eye area.
Flowers: They are used for their antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help soothe and calm the skin around the eyes.
Roots: They are used for their anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can help reduce inflammation and prevent infection in the eye area.
remedy_recipe: Harvest the flowers and leaves of hypericum perforatum in the summer months when they are in full bloom.
Dry the harvested plant material in a warm dark place for 1 to 2 weeks or until brittle.
Store the dried hypericum perforatum in airtight containers to preserve its medicinal properties.
Steep 1 teaspoon of dried hypericum perforatum in 1 cup of boiling water for 5 to 10 minutes.
Strain the tea and drink 1 to 3 cups per day to achieve the desired effect.

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Chamomile tea's anti-inflammatory compounds can reduce puffiness and swelling under the eyes, helping to alleviate dark circles and eye bags.

remedy_recipe: Gather 1 cup of dried matricaria chamomilla flowers from a reputable source or dry fresh flowers yourself.
Steep 1 teaspoon of dried flowers in 8 ounces of boiling water for 5 to 10 minutes.
Strain the tea into a cup using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth to remove the solids.
Discard the solids and add honey or lemon to taste if desired for sweetness or flavor.
Store any leftover tea in the refrigerator for up to 24 hours before reheating and serving again.

remedy_parts: Flowers: They are rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce puffiness and promote relaxation.
Leaves: They have a calming effect and contain properties that help to reduce eye strain and inflammation.

remedy_recipe: Harvest 25-30 fresh rosa damascena flowers at dawn when they are fully open and fragrant.
Dry the flowers in a single layer at room temperature for 7-10 days or until brittle.
Crush 1-2 teaspoons of dried rosa damascena flowers into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle.
Steep 1 teaspoon of the powder in 1 cup of boiling water for 5-7 minutes or until fragrant.
Strain the tea and serve immediately or store it in the refrigerator for up to 24 hours.

remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves are used to make teas for eye bags because they contain calming and anti-inflammatory properties.

remedy_recipe: Harvest 20 to 30 fresh or dried flower buds in the late morning or early afternoon when the lavandula angustifolia is in full bloom.
Rinse the harvested lavandula angustifolia flower buds with filtered water to remove any dirt or debris.
Steep 1 to 2 teaspoons of dried lavandula angustifolia flowers or 3 to 4 fresh flower buds in 8 ounces of boiling water for 5 to 7 minutes.
Strain the tea using a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve to remove the lavandula angustifolia flowers.
Store the brewed herbal tea in a glass bottle in the refrigerator for up to 24 hours before serving.

remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Maypop tea's antioxidant properties help reduce puffiness and dark circles by neutralizing free radicals that can cause inflammation and damage to delicate skin around the eyes.

remedy_recipe: Harvest 1 to 2 ounces of fresh passiflora incarnata leaves and flowers or 1/2 to 1 ounce of dried herb.
Dry the harvested passiflora incarnata for 2 to 3 days to remove excess moisture and preserve the herb.
Steep 1 teaspoon of dried passiflora incarnata or 2 teaspoons of fresh leaves in 8 ounces of boiling water.
Allow the steeped passiflora incarnata to infuse for 5 to 7 minutes to release its active compounds.
Strain the tea and discard the solids to enjoy the herbal tea immediately or store it for later use.

remedy_properties: Adaptogenic effects: Valerian tea helps to calm the body's stress response, which can reduce puffiness and dark circles under the eyes.

Stems: Stems of Valeriana officinalis are sometimes used in teas for their mild sedative properties, which can aid in reducing eye strain and fatigue.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 2-3 ounces of valeriana officinalis roots in the early morning after the plant has dried slightly from the night dew.
Clean the roots thoroughly with distilled water to remove any dirt or debris to avoid contamination.
Chop the cleaned roots into small pieces and dry them in a low-temperature oven at 100°f for 2 hours.
Steep 1 teaspoon of dried root pieces in 8 ounces of boiling water for 5-7 minutes to release the active compounds.
Strain the tea into a cup and discard the solids before serving immediately to enjoy the relaxing effects.

remedy_recipe: Rinse dried marshmallow root with boiling water to remove any impurities and debris.

Steep the mixture in a saucepan or teapot for 5-7 minutes over low heat.
Strain the tea into a cup using a fine-mesh strainer or cheesecloth to remove any solids.

Measure one teaspoon of the herbal blend for every eight ounces of boiling water and add it to a teapot.

remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality dried glycyrrhiza glabra root bark from trusted sources according to standard herbalism practices.
Measure two teaspoons of dried glycyrrhiza glabra root bark and add it to a tea infuser or a heat-resistant cup.
Boil one cup of water in a kettle or pot and pour it over the tea infuser or dried root bark in the cup.
Allow the mixture to steep for 10-15 minutes, then strain the tea into another cup using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth.
Drink the warm tea slowly while still steaming hot, ideally three times daily for best results and eye bag relief.

Strain the tea into a cup using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth to remove the solids.

As a result, regular consumption of stinging nettle tea may leave you with brighter, more radiant eyes that appear less puffy and tired-looking.

Antioxidants: Nettle tea is rich in antioxidants, which neutralize free radicals that can cause oxidative stress and damage to the delicate skin around the eyes, leading to discoloration and bagginess.

Vasodilatory properties: Stinging nettle tea has been shown to have vasodilatory effects, which help improve blood circulation and reduce fluid buildup under the eyes, reducing puffiness and dark circles.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, making them effective in reducing puffiness and dark circles under the eyes.
Seeds: High in omega-3 fatty acids, which help to reduce inflammation and promote healthy skin around the eyes.
remedy_recipe: Harvest urtica dioica leaves and flowers in a shaded area for optimal potency and freshness.
Rinse the harvested plant material under cold running water to remove any dirt or debris.
Chop the washed urtica dioica into small pieces to increase surface area for infusion.
Steep 1 teaspoon of chopped urtica dioica in boiling water for 5-7 minutes to release its medicinal properties.
Strain and drink the tea immediately, allowing the antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds to work on eye bags and puffiness.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lemon balm teas helps with eye bags because of its natural anti-inflammatory properties, which reduce puffiness and soothe tired eyes. remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh melissa officinalis leaves by selecting healthy stems and drying them immediately.
Measure one teaspoon of dried melissa officinalis leaves for every eight ounces of boiling water.
Steep the dried herbs in boiling water for 5-7 minutes, allowing the flavors to infuse thoroughly.
Strain the tea into a cup using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth, discarding solids immediately.
Enjoy the tea warm or at room temperature within 15-20 minutes of preparation for optimal benefits.

remedy_recipe: Harvest tilia platyphyllos flowers and leaves in the early morning for optimal potency and freshness.
Rinse the harvested botanicals gently with cool water to remove dirt and debris.
Steep one teaspoon of dried tilia platyphyllos flowers and two tablespoons of dried leaves in boiling water for five minutes.
Strain the tea mixture through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into a cup, discarding the solids.
Allow the tea to steep for an additional 10-15 minutes before consuming to allow the active compounds to infuse fully.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Wood sorrel teas helps with eye bags because of its potent antioxidant properties, which help to combat inflammation and reduce puffiness. remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh rumex acetosella leaves and rinse them gently under cold running water.
Measure out one tablespoon of dried or two tablespoons of fresh rumex acetosella leaves.
Steep the measured herbs in a tea infuser or strainer in boiling water for 5 to 7 minutes.
Strain the tea into a cup and discard the solids before adding honey to taste if desired.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Hazel teas helps with eye bags because it is rich in antioxidants and flavonoids, which help to reduce inflammation and promote circulation. remedy_recipe: Harvest corylus avellana leaves and flowers from organic gardens or local farmers' markets.
Dry the harvested ingredients using low heat or air drying for 7-10 days to preserve potency.
Combine 1 teaspoon of dried corylus avellana leaves and flowers with boiling water in a teapot.
Steep the mixture for 5-7 minutes, then strain and discard solids while keeping the liquid warm.
Drink the tea immediately or let it cool to room temperature within 15 minutes for optimal benefits.

supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to eye bags that are treated with herbal teas are dark circles, puffiness, and facial swelling. Teas like chamomile, peppermint, and cucumber have anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce these issues. Other herbs like alfalfa, calendula, and green tea can also be used to treat dark spots, hyperpigmentation, and skin discoloration, promoting a more even complexion and reducing the appearance of under-eye circles.
intro_desc: Herbal teas for eye bags are a natural remedy that helps alleviate the appearance of dark circles, puffiness, and discoloration under the eyes. They work by reducing inflammation, improving circulation, and soothing the skin. The best herbal teas for eye bags include Aloe barbadensis, Ginkgo biloba, Rosmarinus officinalis, Hypericum perforatum, Camellia sinensis, Euphrasia officinalis, Echinacea purpurea, Glycyrrhiza glabra, Achillea millefolium, and Silybum marianum. If left untreated, eye bags can negatively impact daily life, causing fatigue, self-consciousness, and difficulty sleeping, ultimately affecting work, social interactions, and overall well-being.
supplementary_best_treatment: The best combination of herbal teas that help with eye bags is a blend of chamomile, peppermint, and lavender. Chamomile soothes and calms the skin, reducing puffiness and inflammation. Peppermint cools and refreshes, constricting blood vessels and minimizing the appearance of dark circles. Lavender promotes relaxation and improves sleep quality, which in turn reduces fluid retention and eye bag formation. Drinking these teas together provides a comprehensive approach to reducing eye bags and achieving a brighter, more rested appearance.

remedies.remedy_desc: Aloe barbadensis teas helps with eye bags because it reduces puffiness and inflammation due to its anti-inflammatory properties. The gel extracted from the aloe plant contains compounds like aloin and aloe-emodin, which have been shown to have a cooling effect on the skin, thereby reducing the appearance of dark circles and eye bags. Additionally, its antioxidant properties help to combat free radicals that can contribute to skin damage and discoloration, leaving the under-eye area looking smoother and more refreshed.
remedy_constituents: Anthraquinones: Help reduce puffiness and inflammation by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes, thus alleviating the appearance of eye bags.
Phenolic acids: Act as antioxidants, neutralizing free radicals that can cause oxidative stress and contribute to the formation of eye bags, thereby reducing their appearance.
Glucomannans: Help reduce fluid retention by improving lymphatic drainage, which in turn decreases the swelling and puffiness associated with eye bags.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Aloe vera leaves are the most commonly used part to make teas for eye bags due to their high concentration of anti-inflammatory compounds.
Gel from Leaves: The gel extracted from the leaves is rich in vitamins, minerals, and antioxidants that help to reduce puffiness and soothe the skin around the eyes.
Pulp from Leaves: The pulp from the leaves is also used to make teas that can help to reduce inflammation and improve circulation, which can help to reduce the appearance of eye bags.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 5-7 leaves of aloe barbadensis with a length of about 6-8 inches for optimal freshness.
Rinse the harvested leaves with cool water to remove any dirt or debris.
Cut the leaves into small pieces and soak them in 2 cups of boiling water for 20 minutes.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve to remove the solids and obtain a clear liquid.
Store the prepared tea in the refrigerator and consume within 3 days for maximum potency.

remedies.remedy_desc: Ginkgo biloba teas helps with eye bags because it improves blood circulation and reduces inflammation in the delicate skin around the eyes. The antioxidants present in Ginkgo biloba tea help to combat free radicals that can cause puffiness and discoloration. By promoting a healthy vascular system, Ginkgo biloba tea also helps to reduce water retention and swelling, providing a more radiant and refreshed appearance. This can leave the skin looking smoother and more even-toned.
remedy_constituents: Bilobalide: Helps reduce puffiness and inflammation associated with eye bags by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes.
Flavonoids (specifically Quercetin and Kaempferol): Act as potent antioxidants that neutralize free radicals, thereby reducing oxidative stress and inflammation that contribute to eye bag formation.
Terpenoids (specifically Ginkgolides A and B): Exhibit anti-inflammatory properties, which help reduce swelling and inflammation in the orbital area, thereby alleviating eye bag symptoms.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are the most commonly used part for teas due to their high concentration of flavonoids and terpenoids, which are believed to reduce inflammation and improve circulation.
Seeds: Ginkgo seeds are also used, particularly for their potential anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help alleviate eye bag issues.

Barks: The barks of the Ginkgo biloba tree contain flavonoids and terpenoids, which are thought to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, making them useful for teas aimed at reducing eye bag appearance.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 100g of ginkgo biloba leaves from a mature tree in late summer or early fall.
Dry the leaves in a single layer at 50°c for 2 hours to remove excess moisture.
Measure out 5-7g of dried ginkgo biloba leaves per cup of boiling water.
Steep the ginkgo biloba leaves in boiling water for 5-7 minutes to release active compounds.
Strain the tea through a fine-mesh sieve and discard the solids before serving.

remedies.remedy_desc: Rosmarinus officinalis teas helps with eye bags because of its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The herbal tea reduces puffiness and dark circles by constricting blood vessels and improving circulation. The antioxidants in rosmarinic acid, a key compound found in rosemary, combat oxidative stress and promote healthy skin. By reducing water retention and inflammation, rosemary tea can leave the under-eye area feeling refreshed and more defined, giving the appearance of reduced eye bags and a more radiant complexion.
remedy_constituents: Rosmarinic acid: This phenolic compound helps reduce eye bags by its antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which minimize puffiness and soothe the skin.

Bornyl acetate: As a terpene, it possesses anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties that help reduce puffiness and alleviate tension around the eyes, thereby reducing the appearance of eye bags.
Carnosic acid: This phenolic compound helps reduce eye bags by its antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which minimize puffiness, soothe the skin, and promote overall eye health.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Rosmarinus officinalis leaves are commonly used due to their high antioxidant properties, which help reduce puffiness and inflammation under the eyes.
Flowers: The flowers of Rosmarinus officinalis are used for their anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which help to reduce swelling and soothe the skin around the eyes.
Stems: Rosmarinus officinalis stems are used for their rich content of rosmarinic acid, which has been shown to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects that can help reduce eye bag appearance.
remedy_recipe: Gather 1 teaspoon of fresh rosmarinus officinalis leaves or 1/2 teaspoon of dried leaves.
Boil 8 ounces of water in a teapot for 2 to 3 minutes at 212 degrees fahrenheit.
Steep the rosmarinus officinalis in the boiling water for 5 to 10 minutes.
Strain the tea through a fine-mesh sieve into a cup or teapot.
Serve the tea hot or iced and enjoy within 15 to 30 minutes.

remedies.remedy_desc: Hypericum perforatum teas helps with eye bags because of its antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties. The tea, also known as St. John's Wort, contains compounds that reduce puffiness and dark circles under the eyes. The flavonoids and phenolic acids in Hypericum perforatum help to improve blood circulation, reduce swelling, and promote the removal of toxins. This leads to a reduction in the appearance of eye bags, leaving the skin looking smoother and more refreshed. Regular consumption of this tea may also help to prevent future occurrences.
remedy_constituents: Hyperforin: Helps to reduce inflammation and swelling associated with eye bags by exhibiting anti-inflammatory properties.
Quercetin: Acts as a powerful antioxidant that helps to reduce puffiness and discoloration by neutralizing free radicals and improving microcirculation.
N-Acetyl Aspartic Acid: Helps to reduce water retention and edema, contributing to a decrease in the appearance of eye bags by promoting the elimination of excess fluids.
remedy_parts: Leaves: High in flavonoids and tannins, which help to reduce puffiness and inflammation.
Flowers: Rich in anthocyanins and other antioxidants that can improve circulation and reduce dark circles.
Stems: Contain bioflavonoids and other compounds that can help to reduce fluid retention and alleviate eye puffiness.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1-2 cups of fresh hypericum perforatum flowers and leaves at dawn or late afternoon when they are dry.
Dry the harvested flowers and leaves in a warm place with good air circulation for 1-2 weeks.
Crush 2-3 teaspoons of dried flowers and leaves into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle.
Steep 1 teaspoon of the crushed powder in boiling water for 5-7 minutes to make the tea.
Strain the tea through a cheesecloth or fine mesh and serve hot or let it cool before drinking.

remedies.remedy_desc: Camellia sinensis teas helps with eye bags because of its high levels of antioxidants and anti-inflammatory properties. The tea's catechin content, particularly EGCG, helps to reduce puffiness and soothe the skin. The antioxidants also combat free radicals, which can contribute to dark circles and swelling under the eyes. Regular consumption of Camellia sinensis tea may also help to improve circulation and promote a more even distribution of fluids, leading to a reduction in eye bags and a more refreshed appearance.
remedy_constituents: Catechins: Catechins have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce puffiness and swelling under the eyes, contributing to a more refreshed appearance.
Quercetin: As an antioxidant and anti-inflammatory compound, quercetin helps alleviate the visible signs of eye bags by reducing inflammation and oxidative stress in the skin.
Theaflavins: Theaflavins possess anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help combat the factors leading to eye bags, such as fluid retention, puffiness, and discoloration.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Camellia sinensis leaves are the most widely used part for making teas for eye bags due to their antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties.
Buds: Camellia sinensis buds are also commonly used for making teas for eye bags due to their ability to soothe and calm the skin.
Leaves (again), but the young leaves: The young leaves of Camellia sinensis are used for making teas for eye bags, especially green tea, which helps to reduce puffiness and dark circles.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1-2 teaspoons of dried camellia sinensis leaves from a reputable source.
Heat 8 ounces of water to a boil in a kettle or teapot for 2-3 minutes.
Steep the camellia sinensis leaves in the boiling water for 3-5 minutes.
Strain the tea mixture through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into a cup.
Serve the herbal camellia sinensis tea immediately and enjoy within 10 minutes.

remedies.remedy_desc: Euphrasia officinalis teas helps with eye bags because of its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The herbal tea contains flavonoids and phenolic acids that soothe and calm the delicate skin around the eyes, reducing puffiness and dark circles. Its astringent properties also help to constrict blood vessels, which can become swollen and cause eye bags. remedy_constituents: Phenylethanoid glycosides: These compounds have anti-inflammatory properties, which help to reduce the swelling and redness associated with eye bags.

Iridoid glycosides: Iridoid glycosides have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which help to protect the delicate skin around the eyes from oxidative stress and inflammation, thereby reducing the appearance of eye bags.
Flavonoids: Flavonoids have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which help to reduce the inflammation and oxidative stress that contribute to the formation of eye bags.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Euphrasia officinalis leaves are used to make teas for eye bags due to their high concentration of bioactive compounds that help reduce inflammation and improve vision.

Flowers: Euphrasia officinalis flowers are used to make teas for eye bags as they contain flavonoids and phenolic acids that have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, beneficial for eye health.
Roots: Euphrasia officinalis roots are used to make teas for eye bags due to their rich content of glycosides and other compounds that help reduce eye strain and improve vision.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1-2 teaspoons of fresh or 1 teaspoon of dried euphrasia officinalis leaves in early morning.
Chop the leaves into small pieces using a knife to release their essential oils.
Steep 1 teaspoon of chopped leaves in 8 ounces of boiling water for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the mixture using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth to remove the leaves.
Drink the tea immediately or store it in the refrigerator for up to 24 hours.

remedies.remedy_desc: Echinacea purpurea teas helps with eye bags because of its potent anti-inflammatory properties, which reduce puffiness and swelling. The antioxidants in Echinacea purpurea also help combat oxidative stress, a key contributor to eye bag formation. Additionally, its ability to boost the immune system and promote lymphatic drainage helps to alleviate fluid buildup under the eyes. remedy_constituents: Iridoids: Help reduce puffiness and inflammation in the eye area by exerting anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects.
Flavonoids: Act as powerful antioxidants that neutralize free radicals, thereby reducing oxidative stress and swelling in the eye area.
Alkylamides: Possess anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce puffiness and alleviate eye strain, promoting a healthier and more refreshed appearance.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of Echinacea purpurea are used to make teas for eye bags because they are rich in compounds that are believed to reduce inflammation and swelling.
Leaves: The leaves are used to make teas for eye bags because they contain antioxidants and other compounds that may help to soothe and calm the skin.
Flowers: The flowers of Echinacea purpurea are used to make teas for eye bags because they are rich in flavonoids and other compounds that may help to reduce puffiness and promote relaxation.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 2-3 ounces of fresh echinacea purpurea roots and flowers in the late summer or early fall season.
Dry the harvested plant material in a warm and dry place for 7-10 days to preserve its medicinal properties.
Measure out 1-2 teaspoons of dried echinacea purpurea root and flowers per 8 ounces of boiling water.
Steep the measured plant material in boiling water for 5-7 minutes to release its active compounds.
Strain the tea and let it cool before serving it in a cup to enjoy its potential health benefits.

remedies.remedy_desc: Glycyrrhiza glabra teas helps with eye bags because it possesses anti-inflammatory properties that reduce puffiness and swelling. The licorice root extract in these teas has been shown to promote lymphatic drainage, which helps to remove excess fluids from the face. Additionally, the antioxidant properties of Glycyrrhiza glabra help to combat oxidative stress, which can contribute to dark circles and eye bags. By drinking Glycyrrhiza glabra teas regularly, individuals may experience a reduction in eye bag appearance and feel more refreshed and revitalized.
remedy_constituents: Glycyrrhizin: Helps reduce inflammation and edema associated with eye bags by its anti-inflammatory properties.
Flavonoids: Contributes to reducing puffiness and dark circles under the eyes due to their antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects.
Licoricidin: Exhibits anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that aid in reducing puffiness and alleviating eye bag discomfort.
remedy_parts: Roots: They contain glycyrrhizin, a natural anti-inflammatory compound that can help reduce puffiness and swelling.
Leaves: They are used for their calming and soothing properties, which can help to relax the eye area and reduce eye bag appearance.
Flowers: They have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help to reduce eye bag swelling and improve overall eye health.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 20-30 dried roots of glycyrrhiza glabra and cut them into smaller pieces weighing approximately 1 ounce.
Steep 1 teaspoon of the cut roots in 8 ounces of boiling water for 5 to 7 minutes.
Strain the liquid through a fine mesh sieve into a cup and discard the solids.
Allow the tea to cool to a comfortable temperature for consumption.
Drink the tea immediately after preparation to experience its full medicinal benefits.

remedies.remedy_desc: Achillea millefolium teas helps with eye bags because it has anti-inflammatory properties that can reduce swelling and puffiness around the eyes. The tea's high content of antioxidants, including flavonoids and phenolic acids, also helps to combat oxidative stress and promote overall eye health. Additionally, the soothing and calming effects of the tea can help to relax the skin, reducing the appearance of dark circles and eye bags, giving users a more refreshed and revitalized look. This makes it an excellent natural remedy for eye care.
remedy_constituents: Apigenin: This flavonoid helps to reduce puffiness and swelling around the eyes due to its anti-inflammatory properties, thereby alleviating the appearance of eye bags.
Nepetalactone: This sesquiterpene lactone has a diuretic effect, which helps to reduce fluid retention and edema, thereby reducing the appearance of eye bags.
Rosmarinic acid: This phenolic acid has antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which help to reduce the inflammation and oxidative stress that contribute to eye bags.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Used due to their anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce puffiness and swelling around the eyes.
Flowers: Used for their antioxidant and soothing effects, which can help calm and relax the skin around the eyes.
Stems: Used for their diuretic properties, which can help reduce water retention and minimize eye puffiness.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1/4 cup of fresh achillea millefolium leaves and flowers in the morning when the plant is at its peak potency.
Dry the harvested plant material in a single layer at room temperature for 2-3 days to preserve its medicinal properties.
Measure out 1 teaspoon of dried achillea millefolium per 8 ounces of boiling water and pour it into a heat-resistant cup.
Steep the mixture for 5-7 minutes to allow the plant's active compounds to infuse into the water.
Strain the tea and discard the solids before serving it warm or at room temperature within 30 minutes.

remedies.remedy_desc: Silybum marianum teas helps with eye bags because it is rich in antioxidants and flavonoids that reduce inflammation and combat free radicals. The plant's active compound, silymarin, has been shown to improve circulation and boost collagen production, which helps to reduce the appearance of dark circles and puffiness. By promoting a healthy and balanced skin environment, Silybum marianum teas can help to minimize the visibility of eye bags, leaving the skin looking smoother and more radiant. Regular consumption may also aid in reducing fluid retention.
remedy_constituents: Flavolignans: Help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress under the eyes, which can contribute to eye bags.
Silymarin: Exert antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects, which can help reduce puffiness and discoloration associated with eye bags.
Polysaccharides: Stimulate collagen production and improve skin elasticity, reducing the appearance of eye bags and dark circles.
remedy_parts: Seeds: They are used to make teas due to their anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce puffiness and swelling around the eyes.
Leaves: The leaves of Silybum marianum are used to make teas that can help reduce water retention and alleviate eye bags.
Flowers: The flowers of this plant are used to make teas that possess anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help reduce puffiness and promote overall eye health.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 30-60g of dried silybum marianum seeds or 60-120g of fresh flowers and leaves.
Cut the dried seeds into smaller pieces or bruise the fresh flowers and leaves.
Heat 250ml of water in a saucepan over medium heat for 5-7 minutes.
Steep the silybum marianum pieces in the hot water for 5-10 minutes.
Strain the tea and discard the solids before serving.
